column9: Early evaluation artifact — see explanation below

column3: The earliest evaluation (notebook 06_evaluate_adversarial) was run immediately after the first RoBERTa training. The results_adversarial.json file shows: paraphraser=T5_Paraphrase_Paws / rewritten_detection_rate=0.066 / rewritten_fooled_rate=0.934. This appears to have been either (a) run against an early model checkpoint before full training converged, OR (b) had different T5 generation parameters (possibly beam search instead of sampling, producing a different attack). These numbers were then incorporated into table03_master_results.csv under the WRONG attack column (labeled Pegasus instead of QuillBot).

When notebook 12 (consolidate_results) assembled table03_master_results.csv, the early nb06 result (which tested T5_Paraphrase_Paws, not Pegasus) appears to have been placed in the Pegasus-Attack row for transformer models. The LR value (0.706 vs 0.710) is close because LR uses different evaluation logic and was likely re-evaluated separately.

The value 0.066 also appears in results_human_evaluation.json as roberta_rewritten_detection=0.066. This refers to RoBERTa's detection rate on the 15 texts used in the human study (tiny sample). This may have been accidentally used to populate the adversarial recall row.

The AUTHORITATIVE numbers are from the final unified evaluation in notebook 10 (all_results_with_hybrids.csv). This notebook evaluated ALL models against ALL three attacks using the same consistent framework, same attack CSV files, and same evaluation code. QuillBot and ChatGPT numbers match across ALL files — only the Pegasus column differs between table03 and the final evaluation, confirming it was an early-stage data loading/labeling error in nb06/nb12.

Use these Pegasus ASR values: RoBERTa 1.2% / BERT 1.8% / DistilBERT 5.2% / Hello-SimpleAI 0.4% / LR 29.0%. These come from all_results_with_hybrids.csv which is the final authoritative source. The Pegasus attack (deterministic beam search) is the LEAST effective attack on transformer models. The earlier numbers in table03 should be disregarded — they reflect an early-stage evaluation error.
